meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with technical debt. Learn how to design and ship professional SaaS applications using AI-driven workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the lack of SaaS knowledge requires moving from manual coding to AI-assisted architectural generation. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid UI scaffolding and Claude Code for backend logic, you can bridge the technical gap, ship enterprise-grade products in days, and maintain full control over your project’s codebase.
content.intro:
The client deadline is looming, the requirement document is a mess, and your lack of deep SaaS infrastructure knowledge feels like a brick wall. You aren't paid to struggle with boilerplate configurations; you are paid to deliver results that look and function like a million-dollar startup product.

This isn't about learning to code from scratch; it's about becoming an AI-augmented architect. By orchestrating Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a struggling developer into a high-velocity product engineer, turning abstract concepts into production-ready SaaS environments before your competition even sets up their dev environment.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Blueprint
steps.description: Establish the core entity relationships and user flows. We use AI to map out the schema before a single line of code is written.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided requirements and generate a high-level SaaS architecture schema including User, Subscription, and core feature entities, formatted for a Next.js/Supabase stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./requirements.md && claude write 'Propose a database schema based on these requirements.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Interface
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the component library and layout structure based on your architecture definition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and ShadcnUI, including a sidebar navigation, data table component, and user settings panel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the UI components based on the Antigravity schema outputs.'

steps.title: Implement Auth and Middleware
steps.description: Handle the complex security layer by letting Claude Code handle the integration of authentication providers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication middleware for Next.js that handles protected routes and session validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement NextAuth.js with Supabase adapter for user authentication.'

steps.title: Connect Data Logic
steps.description: Bridge your frontend to your database. We ensure type safety between the UI components and the backend API.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write server-side API routes for CRUD operations on the user projects table, ensuring full TypeScript type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create API endpoints for the project entity and verify types against the schema.'

steps.title: Refine and Deploy
steps.description: Run final audits, optimize performance, and prepare the build pipeline for deployment to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Conduct a code review on the current project directory and suggest performance optimizations for React components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the codebase for security vulnerabilities and prepare for Vercel deployment.'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'system_prompt.md' in your root directory to keep Claude Code aligned with your preferred coding style.
Use Antigravity to generate documentation comments for every major component; it saves hours of client onboarding time.
When facing a bug, feed the error stack trace directly into Claude Code; it is optimized for rapid debugging loops.
Structure your project with a 'features' folder pattern to keep your codebase modular and easy for AI to navigate.
Run periodic 'clean-up' prompts to remove unused imports and dead code, keeping your project lean and fast.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on integrating a payment provider like Stripe and setting up a basic analytics dashboard to track user engagement. Use your new AI-augmented workflow to iterate based on actual user feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop building the hard way and start scaling your freelance business with AI. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expertise to outpace the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
